original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: British Association of Dermatologists' guidelines for the management of cutaneous warts 2014
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and children with non-genital cutaneous warts, including plantar lesions and persistent or complicated disease.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary care and dermatology assessment and follow-up.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for wart identity, site, duration, persistence, symptoms, functional or cosmetic impact, immune context, examination, and diagnostic uncertainty. Treatment recommendations and procedure parameters are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Terminology, natural history, site, and persistence
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting wart identity, age, lesion site, duration, persistence, multiplicity, immune context, pain, function, and cosmetic impact.

exact_sections.heading: Diagnosis and complications
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-observed morphology and distribution, diagnostic uncertainty, pain, bleeding, secondary infection, and immunosuppression context without inferring diagnosis or treatment.

title: British Association of Dermatologists' guidelines for the management of cutaneous warts 2014
jurisdiction: United Kingdom; specialty guidance requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults and children with non-genital cutaneous warts, including plantar lesions and persistent or complicated disease.
intended_setting: Primary care and dermatology assessment and follow-up.
access_limitations: Official host returned a challenge or shell page rather than guideline content.
